The '''PD-series protocol droid''', sometimes known as the '''PD Lurrian droid''', was a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] produced by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] specifically for the [[Lurrian]]s of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lur]]. The line was designed to appeal to the Lurrians aesthetically and to be able to traverse the harsh conditions of the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Additionally, PD-series droids were programmed with extensive knowledge of genetics and DNA resequencing, making them particularly useful to the Lurrians, who were expert [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticists]]. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] quarantined Lur, Cybot Galactica lost their market. The production run was sold off to a wholesaler at a significant loss and the line become an expensive and embarrassing failure for the manufacturer.

==Description==
The PD-series was designed by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] specifically for the [[Lurrian]] market. The intention was to reap huge profits from designing a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] that could operate in [[Lur]]'s harsh climate; the planet's environment was tough on droid systems so automatons had never caught on as a consumer product.<ref name="EGTD" /> Cybot Galactica designed a revised version of their successful [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]] to meet the needs of the Lurrian market.<ref name="AEG" />

A stock [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] body was used as the base as the Lurrians were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. As the droid had to operate in Lur's cold weather, the [[Powerbus cable|exposed wiring]] common to the 3PO-series was enclosed in [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]] plating, and all of the joints and seams were covered with weather-resistant industrial sealant<ref name="EGTD" /> and impact-cushioning systems.<ref name="FF125" /> PD-series droids were brightly [[Color/Legends|colored]], coming in red, yellow, and orange. The colors allowed the droids to be visible against the backdrop of Lur's snowy climes.<ref name="EGTD" />

[[File:PD-series_EGTD.jpg|left|thumb|PD-series schematics]]
The head was designed to look similarto a Lurrian,<ref name="AEG" /> and incorporated features that would appeal more to the species, including wide, oversized [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]]. A brow ridge jutted out over the droid's photoreceptors to stop them from being obscured by snow. PD droids were equipped with an [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]], but one that had been modified to be of more use to the Lurrians who were expert [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticists]]. Sixty percent of the unit's [[Language/Legends|language]] programming was removed and replaced with an extensive library containing information on genetics and DNA resequencing.<ref name="EGTD" />

Cybot Galactica also incorporated an innovative system into the PD-series that would allow them to traverse the vertical environment of Lur. The planet's gale-force winds made [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] travel extremely dangerous, so Lurrians used a primitive hook and line system to scale their warrenlike [[City/Legends|cities]]. The PD-series were equipped with [[Servogrip|heavy servogrips]] that were detachable and could be fired by [[Engine/Legends|rocket jets]] in the wrist joint. The servogrips had a thin, strong cable attached to them that spooled out from a reel in the droid's forearm,<ref name="EGTD" /> and was capable of reaching up to twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="AEG" /> When the reel retracted, the cable was strong enough to pull the droid up, allowing it to move as the Lurrians did.<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
The PD-series was marketed to the Lurrians, and thousands of units were produced once the Lurrians expressed a strong interest in the design. However, Cybot Galactica was blindsided<ref name="EGTD" /> when [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] decided that the Lurrians and their capabilities posed a sufficient threat to their future plans.<ref name="FF125" /> [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ordered Lur to be quarantined in order to control the planet's genetic specialists.<ref name="EGTD" />

With the Lurrian market now closed to them, Cybot Galactica sold the entire production run to a major wholesaler at a significant loss.<ref name="EGTD" /> The wholesaler did not advertise or market the series well, and many of the droids that were sold ended up not working to their full capabilities.<ref name="FF125" /> The PD-series were still fully capable protocol droids, and were particularly useful to individuals with an interest in genetics<ref name="EGTD" /> or [[Biologist/Legends|biology]].<ref name="AEG" /> 

Their robust design meant that PD droids rarely broke down, with many of the initial production run still operating long after their construction.<ref name="AEG" /> Droids sold to owners that were not Lurrian or geneticists often had their modified [[Droid brain/Legends|brain]] replaced.<ref name="FF125" /> The failure of the PD series was a costly and embarrassing disaster for Cybot Galactica despite it not being the company's fault.<ref name="EGTD" />

[[PDA6]] was a PD-series droid owned by [[Zorneth]], an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] who co-developed the [[savorium herb]]. The droid served Zorneth as his personal assistant aboard his [[Zorneth's Herdship|herdship]].<ref name="EGTD" />
